item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hello,My name is C-take.
I'm member in SCP Foundation JP-Branch.

I was attracted by this article.
Therefore I just try to translate this article into Japanese.
Can you tell me the source of SCP-1362's image(photo)?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as described below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a chance to speak with Roget about his headcanon regarding these ill-fated explorers, and as it turns out they may potentially come from the universe mentioned in SCP-1483 given the similarities of environment the cartographers describe in the Greenland they see, and that described in Antarctica in 1483. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Date: ██/██/19██
Signal: Whole place is trees forever. Land is primarily barren, cold, useless.
Note: No instances of SCP-1362-1 had left SCP-1362.

To this:

Date: ██/██/19██
Signal: Whole place is trees forever. Land is primarily barren, cold, useless.
Note: No instances of SCP-1362-1 had left SCP-1362.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the easiest SCP to document, it literally does it for you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article is intriguingly weird to begin with, but I lost all interest in it half-way through. The thing with the smoke messages and the entities involved being marooned doesn't grab me at all, and seems like something that's been done a few times already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cube is the actual intelligence here, the humanoids connect to it and compile data within it. The reason it's a cube is that it was never intended to be the interesting part, which is why 99% of this focuses on the humanoids.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall I quite like this one. My only big complaint would be the use of the mysterious faceless cube which has gotten to be a very generic concept and doesn't really add anything here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All those years, and Nobody ever tried to give them a ride? Foundation, or otherwise? You skipped the only interesting part

item.title: Re: Error Made
item.content:encoded: You can edit your posts, no need to double post.

item.title: Re: Incomplete
item.content:encoded:
You can click 'options' under a post to edit it instead of double-posting.

It's like fearing someone just because they're different.

That's actually half of the Foundation's purpose. It's called "protecting normalcy". We, as a race, have certain beliefs about how our world works, what's possible, and what exists within it. An anomaly such as SCP-1362 breaks that normalcy, and that's why the Foundation must step in to keep knowledge of it hidden. If word of its existence got out, it might spread panic. That's the unspoken, underlying rule of why containment exists in the first place. Not all SCPs have to be dangerous or scary.

item.title: Error Made
item.content:encoded: Meant to say the Smoke or the Cartographer's not the same thing twice.

item.title: Incomplete
item.content:encoded: This post seems very undone. First of the ONLY THING that was mentioned that the SCP does is act weird and try to find transport by rather human like needs or by circling the car. Nowhere does it say why it is REQUIRED you can't see the smoke signals or the Cartographer's smoke lest you get taken in for questioning and given a Class C amnesiac. Is the SCP dangerous? No. Then why are they using such thick measures? It's like fearing someone just because they're different. Granted these guys are so different they aren't the same species but still, you never gave any proof as to WHY they're being so tight on security.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it.

The only criticism I have is; the hole in the top of the cube should have a stated diameter.
Scientists are very particular about a technical detail like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should the caption read 1362-A instead of -1?

Edit: never mind. I get it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love that -B just rolls around everywhere. The last addendum was great. Also, lots of anomalous activity in Greenland lately!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really didn't believe that this article would work out, but it did! well done all +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a relatively obscure concept there seems to be a lot of articles with a similar twist. +1 though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the cube graphite of granite?

I don't like it much. -F is interesting, but the rest of them - not so much, and the whole thing doesn't tie together all that well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good enough for an upvote, but I'd really like to learn more about 1362-F. He seems oddly unscientific to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The addendum sold it for me. Great use of implication over explicit exposition. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the idea that extraterrestrials thought the Earth was unsuitable after looking at one spot, and then those that were sent to look were left behind. Yet, now I'm wondering what Scantron looked at.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fixed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but this line should be fixed:

A cube composed of graphite, measuring 12cm x 12cm x 12cm, has been placed at the center of SCP-1362, with stress testing showing it to be composed of ordinary material. A small circle has been engraved into the top of this cube.

The use of "had been" implies it was the Foundation or some other agency external to the SCP that had placed it there. I think that saying "is located at" and "is" would work better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure which version Scantron saw, but I like this one a lot. It ties together better than the phantom cabman incarnation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the last draft you showed me better. This is weird for the sake of weird with some stuff about being stranded tacked on that feels neither original nor subtle.
